my_thoughts: The criterion/analogy works: mastery = can discuss without coloring (leakage), defines: leakage = expression (energy loss), analogy (balloon: leaks = loses air), links: silence = accumulation (conscious energy). The student reported difficulty (feeling compelled to express), which shows they understand: discussion = colored by knowledge (internal coloring). They'll likely accept the criterion/analogy. The episode is complete: altruistic impulse → denial/prediction → expression → criterion/analogy. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student expressed altruistic impulse (impulse to evangelize)—denial/prediction: cannot use knowledge, distortion + negative consequences. T2: Student reported difficulty ('It is difficult to discuss something with anyone without it being coloured by what we have heard here')—feeling compelled to express via reports difficulty and describes internal coloring. They understand: discussion = colored by knowledge (internal coloring). The structural_analogy mechanism sets criterion: mastery = can discuss without coloring (leakage), defines: leakage = expression (energy loss), analogy (balloon: leaks = loses air), links: silence = accumulation (conscious energy). Pattern complete: altruistic impulse → denial/prediction → expression report → criterion/analogy. They've shifted: expression = leakage (energy loss), silence = accumulation (criterion/analogy).
visible_response:
Until it becomes easy you cannot begin to do anything;
everything will be
transformed into talk and will remain talk. Only when you are able to keep silent, keep
something to yourself, only then can you accumulate more knowledge or material.
If
you make a hole in a balloon, the contents of the balloon will escape.
If you make a
hole in yourself, something will also escape.
Rules are difficult to keep, because by
remembering rules and obeying rules you accumulate conscious energy.
